stem: During the first part of an experiment, a model rocket was launched from a 12-meter-tall platform. A graph shows its height y, in meters, x seconds after launch, and the graph passes through (0, 12). During the second part, the same rocket was launched the same way but from a platform 3 meters shorter. Which of the following describes a graph that could represent the height y, in meters, of the rocket x seconds after launch during the second part?

answerOptions.content: A graph with the same shape as the first, shifted down 3 units, passing through (0, 9).
A graph with the same shape as the first, shifted up 3 units, passing through (0, 15).
A graph with the same shape as the first, shifted left 3 units.
A graph with the same shape as the first, shifted right 3 units.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. The launch platform is 3 meters shorter, so the rocket starts 3 meters lower than before. Every height value is therefore 3 less than the corresponding height in the first part, giving a graph translated 3 units downward, with y-intercept (0, 9).

Choice B raises the height, the opposite of what is described. Choices C and D shift the graph horizontally, which would change the time of launch, not the initial height.
